LAW AND MOTION 302, A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: PLAINTIFF BARBARA JAGIELLO'S MOTION TO DISQUALIFY JOHN MOUNIER TO ACT AS COUNSEL IN THIS MATTER IS GRANTED. MR. MOUNIER'S PARTICIPATION IN THE PREPARATION OF THE RETAINER AGREEMENT AND HIS WORK WITH AND/OR ON BEHALF OF MS. JAGIELLO CONCERNING MS. JAGIELLO'S PROPOSED REPRESENTATION OF MS. CHRISTMAN REQUIRES THAT MR. MOUNIER BE DISQUALIFIED EVEN IN THE ABSENCE OF ANY SHOWING THAT MS. JAGIELLO COMMUNICATED INFORMATION IN CONFIDENCE TO MR. MOUNIER WHICH MR. MOUNIER AGREED TO, OR SHOULD HAVE UNDERSTOOD WAS, NOT TO BE DISCLOSED TO MS. CHRISTMAN. THE DEMURRER AND MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT SCHEDULED ON FEBRUARY 6 IS CONTINUED TO MARCH 21, 2012 PER AGREEMENT OF THE PARTIES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LA VENA WARD, CSR # 7077

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. CROSS-DEFENDANT BARBARA JAGIELLO'S DEMURRER TO DEFENDANT, SUSAN ELIZABETH CHRISTMAN'S 4TH AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE UCL AND BREACH OF CONTRACT CLAIMS AND OVERRULED AS TO THE CONVERSION CLAIM. THESE ARE DEMURRERS TO THE FOURTH AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. THE EARLIER ORDER OF THIS COURT DATED APRIL 26, 2012 LIKELY CONTAINED AN ERROR IN NOT DELETING 1 (C) FROM THE FORM OFFERED BY COUNSEL, IN THAT THE PRIOR DEMURRER WAS OVERRULED ON THIS CLAIM. ACCORDINGLY THE DEMURER TO THE CONVERSION CLAIM IS OVERRULED. CONTRARY TO THE COURT PRIOR ORDER, CHRISTMAN FAILS TO SPECIFICALLY ALLEGE THE AMOUNT OF MONEY PAID IN ACTUAL RELIANCE ON JAGIELLO'S ALLEGED UNFAIR CONDUCT. CHRISTMAN, THEREFORE, LACKS STANDING TO BRING THE UCL CLAIM. CHRISTMAN NOW ALLEGES SHE VOIDED THE CONTRACT AND IN THE ABSENCE OF ANOTHER ALLEGED CONTRACT BETWEEN THE PARTIES THE BREACH OF CONTRACT CLAIM CANNOT LIE. INSTEAD OF COMPLYING WITH THE COURT'S EARLIER ORDER, THE PUTATIVE CONTRACT CLAIM APPEARS TO INVOKE, INSTEAD, OTHER TYPES OF CLAIMS SUCH AS B&P 6148. AT THIS LATE STAGE IN THE MULTIPLE DEMURRER PROCEEDINGS, IT DOES NOT APPEAR THAT ANY OF THESE DEFICIENCIES CAN BE CURED. THE REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE CURTIS KARNOW; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED.

DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-01-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. THOMAS HUSTER, TEL. NO. 984-9915 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANT BARBARA JAGIELLO'S DEMURRER TO DEFENDANT, SUSAN ELIZABETH CHRISTMAN'S 4TH AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is Sustained without leave to amend as to the UCL and breach of contract claims and overruled as to the conversion claim. These are demurrers to the fourth amended cross complaint. The earlier order of this Court dated April 26, 2012 likely contained an error in not deleting 1 (c) from the form offered by counsel, in that the prior demurrer was overruled on this claim. Accordingly the demurer to the conversion claim is overruled. Contrary to the court prior order, Christman fails to specifically allege the amount of money paid in actual reliance on Jagiello's alleged unfair conduct. Christman, therefore, lacks standing to bring the UCL claim. Christman now alleges she voided the contract and in the absence of another alleged contract between the parties the breach of contract claim cannot lie. Instead of complying with the court's earlier order, the putative contract claim appears to invoke, instead, other types of claims such as B&P 6148. At this late stage in the multiple demurrer proceedings, it does not appear that any of these deficiencies can be cured. The request for judicial notice is denied. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge Curtis Karnow; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/CK

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Disqualify John Mounier To Act As Counsel In This Matter
Off Calendar Jan-26-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. THOMAS HUSTER, TEL. NO. 984-9915 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
JOHN F. MOUNIER JR, TEL. NO. 285-0158 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: PLAINTIFF BARBARA JAGIELLO'S Motion To Disqualify John Mounier To Act As Counsel In This Matter IS Granted. Mr. Mounier's participation in the preparation of the retainer agreement and his work with and/or on behalf of Ms. Jagiello concerning Ms. Jagiello's proposed representation of Ms. Christman requires that Mr. Mounier be disqualified even in the absence of any showing that Ms. Jagiello communicated information in confidence to Mr. Mounier which Mr. Mounier agreed to, or should have understood was, not to be disclosed to Ms. Christman. THE DEMURRER AND MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT SCHEDULED ON FEBRUARY 6 IS CONTINUED TO MARCH 21, 2012 PER AGREEMENT OF THE PARTIES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: La Vena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (302/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion Compelling Responses To Request For Admission And Related Form Of Interrogatory 17.1 For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-26-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Huster (415-984-9915) of Law Offices of Thomas Huster for Barbara A. Jagiello, plaintiff and moving party / John E. Moynier (415-289-0158) of APS Animal Legal Protection for Elisabeth Christman, defendant and moving party

ARGUED - Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:
PLAINTIFF BARBARA JAGIELLO Notice Of Motion And Motion Compelling Responses To Request For Admission And Related Form Of Interrogatory 17.1 For Monetary Sanctions. Motion denied. The Court finds that the parties did not engage in meaningful meet and confer. The Court urges the parties to do so in the future. Any request for sanctions is denied. Prevailing party to prepare order. JUDGE PROTEM ELISABETH RILES, NOT REPORTED =(302/JPT)
